var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

# Load the data
businesses = var_functions.query_db:44
reviews = var_functions.query_db:42

print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))
print('Total review summaries:', len(reviews))

# Filter businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_biz = [biz for biz in businesses if isinstance(biz.get('attributes'), dict) and biz.get('attributes').get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True']
print('Credit card businesses:', len(credit_card_biz))

# Extract categories
cat_counter = Counter()
biz_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Find categories using regex
    match = None
    for pattern in [r'services in ([^\.]+)', r'including ([^\.]+)', r'fields of ([^\.]+)']:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            cats = [c.strip() for c in re.split(r',| and ', cat_text) if c.strip()]
            clean_cats = [c.strip('.,') for c in cats if len(c.strip('.,')) > 1]
            
            for cat in clean_cats:
                cat_counter[cat] += 1
                if biz_id not in biz_categories:
                    biz_categories[biz_id] = []
                biz_categories[biz_id].append(cat)
            break

# Get results
top_cat, top_count = cat_counter.most_common(1)[0] if cat_counter else (None, 0)
print('Top category:', top_cat)
print('Count:', top_count)

# Get business IDs for top category
top_biz_ids = [biz_id for biz_id, cats in biz_categories.items() if top_cat in cats]
print('Business IDs sample:', top_biz_ids[:5])

# Convert business IDs to review format (businessref_X)
review_biz_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]
print('Review format IDs sample:', review_biz_ids[:5])

# Calculate average rating
rating_sum = 0
rating_count = 0
for review in reviews:
    if review.get('business_ref') in review_biz_ids:
        rating_sum += float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
        rating_count += 1

avg_rating = rating_sum / rating_count if rating_count > 0 else 0
print('Businesses with ratings:', rating_count)
print('Average rating:', avg_rating)

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Access business data
businesses = var_functions.query_db:44
reviews = var_functions.query_db:42

print('Total businesses loaded:', len(businesses))
print('Total review summaries loaded:', len(reviews))

# Filter businesses accepting credit cards
credit_card_biz = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

print('Businesses accepting credit cards:', len(credit_card_biz))

# Categories extraction
all_categories = []
business_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Look for various patterns
    pattern_found = False
    
    # Method 1: services in
    pos = desc.find('services in ')
    if pos > 0:
        start = pos + len('services in ')
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            cat_text = desc[start:end]
            categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
            pattern_found = True
    
    # Method 2: including
    if not pattern_found:
        pos = desc.find('including ')
        if pos > 0:
            start = pos + len('including ')
            end = desc.find('.', start)
            if end > start:
                cat_text = desc[start:end]
                categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
                pattern_found = True
    
    # Method 3: fields of
    if not pattern_found:
        pos = desc.find('fields of ')
        if pos > 0:
            start = pos + len('fields of ')
            end = desc.find('.', start)
            if end > start:
                cat_text = desc[start:end]
                categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
                pattern_found = True
    
    if pattern_found:
        clean_cats = []
        for cat in categories:
            cat = cat.strip()
            if len(cat) > 1:
                cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
                clean_cats.append(cat)
                all_categories.append(cat)
        
        if clean_cats:
            business_categories[biz_id] = clean_cats

# Count categories
category_counts = Counter(all_categories)
print('Categories extracted:', len(category_counts))

# Get top category
if category_counts:
    top_cat_name = category_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
    top_cat_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0][1]
    print('Top category:', top_cat_name)
    print('Businesses in top category:', top_cat_count)
    
    # Get business IDs
    top_biz_ids = []
    for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
        if top_cat_name in cats:
            top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)
    
    print('Sample business IDs:', top_biz_ids[:3])
    
    # Convert to review business_ref format
    review_biz_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]
    print('Sample review IDs:', review_biz_ids[:3])
    
    # Calculate average rating
    total_rating = 0.0
    rating_count = 0
    
    for review in reviews:
        biz_ref = review.get('business_ref')
        if biz_ref in review_biz_ids:
            total_rating += float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
            rating_count += 1
    
    if rating_count > 0:
        avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count
        print('Businesses with ratings found:', rating_count)
        print('Average rating:', round(avg_rating, 2))
    else:
        print('No ratings found for this category')
else:
    print('No categories found')

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

print('Businesses accepting credit cards:', len(credit_card_biz))

# Categories extraction
all_categories = []
business_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Look for various patterns
    pattern_found = False
    
    # Method 1: services in
    pos = desc.find('services in ')
    if pos > 0:
        start = pos + len('services in ')
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            cat_text = desc[start:end]
            categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
            pattern_found = True
    
    # Method 2: including
    if not pattern_found:
        pos = desc.find('including ')
        if pos > 0:
            start = pos + len('including ')
            end = desc.find('.', start)
            if end > start:
                cat_text = desc[start:end]
                categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
                pattern_found = True
    
    # Method 3: fields of
    if not pattern_found:
        pos = desc.find('fields of ')
        if pos > 0:
            start = pos + len('fields of ')
            end = desc.find('.', start)
            if end > start:
                cat_text = desc[start:end]
                categories = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
                pattern_found = True
    
    if pattern_found:
        clean_cats = []
        for cat in categories:
            cat = cat.strip()
            if len(cat) > 1:
                cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
                clean_cats.append(cat)
                all_categories.append(cat)
        
        if clean_cats:
            business_categories[biz_id] = clean_cats

# Count categories
category_counts = Counter(all_categories)
print('Categories extracted:', len(category_counts))

# Get top category
if category_counts:
    top_cat_name = category_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
    top_cat_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0][1]
    print('Top category:', top_cat_name)
    print('Businesses in top category:', top_cat_count)
    
    # Get business IDs
    top_biz_ids = []
    for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
        if top_cat_name in cats:
            top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)
    
    print('Sample business IDs:', top_biz_ids[:3])
    
    # Convert to review business_ref format
    review_biz_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]
    print('Sample review IDs:', review_biz_ids[:3])
    
    # Calculate average rating
    total_rating = 0.0
    rating_count = 0
    
    for review in reviews:
        biz_ref = review.get('business_ref')
        if biz_ref in review_biz_ids:
            total_rating += float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
            rating_count += 1
    
    if rating_count > 0:
        avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count
        print('Businesses with ratings found:', rating_count)
        print('Average rating:', round(avg_rating, 2))
    else:
        print('No ratings found for this category')
else:
    print('No categories found')

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.query: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review GROUP BY business_ref

exec_args.sql: SELECT business_ref, AVG(CAST(rating AS FLOAT)) as avg_rating, COUNT(*) as review_count FROM review GROUP BY business_ref

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses and reviews from storage
businesses = var_functions.query_db:62
reviews = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))
print('Total review summaries:', len(reviews))

# 1. Filter businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_biz = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

print('Businesses accepting credit cards:', len(credit_card_biz))

# 2. Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract category list using regex patterns
    cat_text = ''
    
    # Try different patterns
    if 'services in' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'including' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'fields of' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    
    if cat_text:
        # Split by comma and 'and', clean up
        categories = re.split(r', | and |, and ', cat_text)
        clean_categories = []
        
        for cat in categories:
            cat = cat.strip()
            if cat and len(cat) > 1:
                # Remove trailing punctuation
                cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
                clean_categories.append(cat)
                category_counts[cat] += 1
                
        if clean_categories:
            business_categories[biz_id] = clean_categories

# 3. Find top category
if category_counts:
    top_category, top_category_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    print('\nTop category:', top_category)
    print('Number of businesses:', top_category_count)
    
    # 4. Get business IDs for top category
    top_business_ids = []
    for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
        if top_category in cats:
            top_business_ids.append(biz_id)
    
    print('Sample business IDs:', top_business_ids[:5])
    print('Total businesses in top category:', len(top_business_ids))
    
    # 5. Convert to review format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
    review_business_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_business_ids]
    
    # 6. Calculate average rating for these businesses
    total_rating = 0
    rating_business_count = 0
    ratings_found = []
    
    for review in reviews:
        if review.get('business_ref') in review_business_ids:
            rating = float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
            total_rating += rating
            rating_business_count += 1
            ratings_found.append(rating)
    
    if rating_business_count > 0:
        avg_rating = total_rating / rating_business_count
        print('\nAverage rating for %s:' % top_category)
        print('Number of businesses with ratings:', rating_business_count)
        print('Average rating:', round(avg_rating, 2))
    else:
        print('\nNo ratings found for businesses in this category')
        avg_rating = 0
else:
    print('No categories found')
    top_category = None
    top_category_count = 0
    avg_rating = 0

# Prepare final results
result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_category_count if top_category else 0,
    'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating > 0 else None
}

print('\nFinal result:', result)

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses and reviews from storage
businesses = var_functions.query_db:62
reviews = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Total businesses:', len(businesses))
print('Total review summaries:', len(reviews))

# 1. Filter businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_biz = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

print('Businesses accepting credit cards:', len(credit_card_biz))

# 2. Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract category list using regex patterns
    cat_text = ''
    
    # Try different patterns
    if 'services in' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'including' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'fields of' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    
    if cat_text:
        # Split by comma and 'and', clean up
        categories = re.split(r', | and |, and ', cat_text)
        clean_categories = []
        
        for cat in categories:
            cat = cat.strip()
            if cat and len(cat) > 1:
                # Remove trailing punctuation
                cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
                clean_categories.append(cat)
                category_counts[cat] += 1
                
        if clean_categories:
            business_categories[biz_id] = clean_categories

# 3. Find top category
if category_counts:
    top_category, top_category_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    print('\nTop category:', top_category)
    print('Number of businesses:', top_category_count)
    
    # 4. Get business IDs for top category
    top_business_ids = []
    for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
        if top_category in cats:
            top_business_ids.append(biz_id)
    
    print('Sample business IDs:', top_business_ids[:5])
    print('Total businesses in top category:', len(top_business_ids))
    
    # 5. Convert to review format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
    review_business_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_business_ids]
    
    # 6. Calculate average rating for these businesses
    total_rating = 0
    rating_business_count = 0
    ratings_found = []
    
    for review in reviews:
        if review.get('business_ref') in review_business_ids:
            rating = float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
            total_rating += rating
            rating_business_count += 1
            ratings_found.append(rating)
    
    if rating_business_count > 0:
        avg_rating = total_rating / rating_business_count
        print('\nAverage rating for %s:' % top_category)
        print('Number of businesses with ratings:', rating_business_count)
        print('Average rating:', round(avg_rating, 2))
    else:
        print('\nNo ratings found for businesses in this category')
        avg_rating = 0
else:
    print('No categories found')
    top_category = None
    top_category_count = 0
    avg_rating = 0

# Prepare final results
result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_category_count if top_category else 0,
    'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating > 0 else None
}

print('\nFinal result:', result)

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load businesses and reviews
businesses = var_functions.query_db:62
reviews = var_functions.query_db:64

# Find businesses accepting credit cards
credit_card_biz = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

# Extract categories from descriptions
all_categories = []
biz_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    categories = []
    
    pattern = None
    if 'services in' in desc:
        pattern = r'services in ([^\.]+)'
    elif 'including' in desc:
        pattern = r'including ([^\.]+)'
    elif 'fields of' in desc:
        pattern = r'fields of ([^\.]+)'
    
    if pattern:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            cats = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
            for cat in cats:
                cat_clean = cat.strip().rstrip('.,')
                if len(cat_clean) > 1:
                    categories.append(cat_clean)
                    all_categories.append(cat_clean)
    
    if categories:
        biz_categories[biz.get('business_id')] = categories

cat_counts = Counter(all_categories)

top_cat = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
top_cat_count = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0][1]

top_biz_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in biz_categories.items():
    if top_cat in cats:
        top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)

review_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]

total_rating = 0
rating_count = 0
for review in reviews:
    if review.get('business_ref') in review_ids:
        total_rating += float(review.get('avg_rating'))
        rating_count += 1

avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count if rating_count > 0 else 0

result = {}
result['top_category'] = top_cat
result['business_count'] = top_cat_count
result['avg_rating'] = round(avg_rating, 2)

print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes', {})
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_biz.append(biz)

# Extract categories from descriptions
all_categories = []
biz_categories = {}

for biz in credit_card_biz:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    categories = []
    
    pattern = None
    if 'services in' in desc:
        pattern = r'services in ([^\.]+)'
    elif 'including' in desc:
        pattern = r'including ([^\.]+)'
    elif 'fields of' in desc:
        pattern = r'fields of ([^\.]+)'
    
    if pattern:
        match = re.search(pattern, desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
            cats = re.split(r', | and ', cat_text)
            for cat in cats:
                cat_clean = cat.strip().rstrip('.,')
                if len(cat_clean) > 1:
                    categories.append(cat_clean)
                    all_categories.append(cat_clean)
    
    if categories:
        biz_categories[biz.get('business_id')] = categories

cat_counts = Counter(all_categories)

top_cat = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
top_cat_count = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0][1]

top_biz_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in biz_categories.items():
    if top_cat in cats:
        top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)

review_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]

total_rating = 0
rating_count = 0
for review in reviews:
    if review.get('business_ref') in review_ids:
        total_rating += float(review.get('avg_rating'))
        rating_count += 1

avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count if rating_count > 0 else 0

result = {}
result['top_category'] = top_cat
result['business_count'] = top_cat_count
result['avg_rating'] = round(avg_rating, 2)

print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load all businesses
print('Loading business data...')
businesses = var_functions.query_db:62
print(f'Total businesses: {len(businesses)}')

# Filter businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_businesses.append(biz)

print(f'Businesses accepting credit cards: {len(credit_card_businesses)}')

# Extract categories from descriptions
all_categories = []
business_category_map = {}

for biz in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    categories = []
    
    # Try to extract category list from description
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z"
    
    # Method 1: services in ...
    match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
    else:
        # Method 2: including ...
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
        else:
            # Method 3: fields of ...
            match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                cat_text = match.group(1)
            else:
                continue
    
    # Clean and split categories
    cat_text = cat_text.strip()
    if cat_text.endswith('.'):
        cat_text = cat_text[:-1]
    
    # Split by comma and 'and'
    categories = [c.strip() for c in re.split(r',\s+|\sand\s', cat_text) if c.strip()]
    
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
        if len(cat) > 1:
            all_categories.append(cat)
            if biz_id not in business_category_map:
                business_category_map[biz_id] = []
            business_category_map[biz_id].append(cat)

category_counts = Counter(all_categories)
print(f'Categories extracted: {len(category_counts)}')

# Get top category
top_category, top_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f'Top category: {top_category} with {top_count} businesses')

# Get business IDs in the top category
top_business_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in business_category_map.items():
    if top_category in cats:
        top_business_ids.append(biz_id)

print(f'Total businesses in top category: {len(top_business_ids)}')
print(f'Sample business IDs: {top_business_ids[:3]}')

# Convert to review format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
review_business_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_business_ids]
print(f'Sample review IDs: {review_business_ids[:3]}')

# Calculate average rating for these businesses
print('Loading review data...')
reviews = var_functions.query_db:64

# Get individual reviews for these businesses
print('Fetching individual reviews...')
reviews_result = var_functions.query_db:70
individual_reviews = []

# Query for all reviews for the businesses in our top category
# We need to query the review table to get all reviews for these specific businesses
import sys
print('System ready to query individual reviews')

result_summary = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'top_business_ids': top_business_ids[:5],
    'review_business_ids': review_business_ids[:5]
}

print(json.dumps(result_summary, indent=2))

If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load all businesses
print('Loading business data...')
businesses = var_functions.query_db:62
print(f'Total businesses: {len(businesses)}')

# Filter businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for biz in businesses:
    attrs = biz.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict) and attrs.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
        credit_card_businesses.append(biz)

print(f'Businesses accepting credit cards: {len(credit_card_businesses)}')

# Extract categories from descriptions
all_categories = []
business_category_map = {}

for biz in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    categories = []
    
    # Try to extract category list from description
    # Look for patterns like "services in X, Y, and Z" or "including X, Y, and Z"
    
    # Method 1: services in ...
    match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(1)
    else:
        # Method 2: including ...
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
        else:
            # Method 3: fields of ...
            match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
            if match:
                cat_text = match.group(1)
            else:
                continue
    
    # Clean and split categories
    cat_text = cat_text.strip()
    if cat_text.endswith('.'):
        cat_text = cat_text[:-1]
    
    # Split by comma and 'and'
    categories = [c.strip() for c in re.split(r',\s+|\sand\s', cat_text) if c.strip()]
    
    for cat in categories:
        cat = cat.rstrip('.,')
        if len(cat) > 1:
            all_categories.append(cat)
            if biz_id not in business_category_map:
                business_category_map[biz_id] = []
            business_category_map[biz_id].append(cat)

category_counts = Counter(all_categories)
print(f'Categories extracted: {len(category_counts)}')

# Get top category
top_category, top_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f'Top category: {top_category} with {top_count} businesses')

# Get business IDs in the top category
top_business_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in business_category_map.items():
    if top_category in cats:
        top_business_ids.append(biz_id)

print(f'Total businesses in top category: {len(top_business_ids)}')
print(f'Sample business IDs: {top_business_ids[:3]}')

# Convert to review format (businessid_X -> businessref_X)
review_business_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_business_ids]
print(f'Sample review IDs: {review_business_ids[:3]}')

# Calculate average rating for these businesses
print('Loading review data...')
reviews = var_functions.query_db:64

# Get individual reviews for these businesses
print('Fetching individual reviews...')
reviews_result = var_functions.query_db:70
individual_reviews = []

# Query for all reviews for the businesses in our top category
# We need to query the review table to get all reviews for these specific businesses
import sys
print('System ready to query individual reviews')

result_summary = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'top_business_ids': top_business_ids[:5],
    'review_business_ids': review_business_ids[:5]
}

print(json.dumps(result_summary, indent=2))

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Get businesses that accept credit cards
cc_businesses = var_functions.query_db:76

print(f"Total credit card businesses: {len(cc_businesses)}")

# Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

for biz in cc_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract category text
    cat_text = ''
    if 'services in' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'including' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'fields of' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    
    if cat_text:
        if cat_text.endswith('.'):
            cat_text = cat_text[:-1]
        
        # Split and clean categories
        categories = [c.strip().rstrip('.,') for c in re.split(r',\s+|\sand\s', cat_text) if c.strip()]
        
        business_categories[biz_id] = categories
        for cat in categories:
            if len(cat) > 1:
                category_counts[cat] += 1

# Get top category
top_category, top_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f"Top category: {top_category} ({top_count} businesses)")

# Get business IDs in top category
top_biz_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
    if top_category in cats:
        top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)

print(f"Total businesses confirmed in top category: {len(top_biz_ids)}")

# Convert to review format
review_biz_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]

# Get review data
review_averages = var_functions.query_db:64

# Calculate average rating
total_rating = 0.0
rating_count = 0
rated_businesses = []

for review in review_averages:
    biz_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if biz_ref in review_biz_ids:
        avg_rating = float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
        total_rating += avg_rating
        rating_count += 1
        rated_businesses.append((biz_ref, avg_rating))

avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count if rating_count > 0 else 0

print(f"Businesses with ratings found: {rating_count}")
print(f"Average rating: {avg_rating:.2f}")

# Verify with a few examples
print("\nSample businesses in top category with ratings:")
for i, (biz_ref, rating) in enumerate(rated_businesses[:3]):
    biz_id = biz_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    biz_name = next((b['name'] for b in cc_businesses if b['business_id'] == biz_id), 'Unknown')
    print(f"  {biz_name}: {rating:.2f}")

result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
}

print('\n__RESULT__:', json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Get businesses that accept credit cards
cc_businesses = var_functions.query_db:76

print(f"Total credit card businesses: {len(cc_businesses)}")

# Extract categories from descriptions
category_counts = Counter()
business_categories = {}

for biz in cc_businesses:
    desc = biz.get('description', '')
    biz_id = biz.get('business_id', '')
    
    # Extract category text
    cat_text = ''
    if 'services in' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'services in ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'including' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'including ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    elif 'fields of' in desc.lower():
        match = re.search(r'fields of ([^\.]+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
        if match:
            cat_text = match.group(1)
    
    if cat_text:
        if cat_text.endswith('.'):
            cat_text = cat_text[:-1]
        
        # Split and clean categories
        categories = [c.strip().rstrip('.,') for c in re.split(r',\s+|\sand\s', cat_text) if c.strip()]
        
        business_categories[biz_id] = categories
        for cat in categories:
            if len(cat) > 1:
                category_counts[cat] += 1

# Get top category
top_category, top_count = category_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f"Top category: {top_category} ({top_count} businesses)")

# Get business IDs in top category
top_biz_ids = []
for biz_id, cats in business_categories.items():
    if top_category in cats:
        top_biz_ids.append(biz_id)

print(f"Total businesses confirmed in top category: {len(top_biz_ids)}")

# Convert to review format
review_biz_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in top_biz_ids]

# Get review data
review_averages = var_functions.query_db:64

# Calculate average rating
total_rating = 0.0
rating_count = 0
rated_businesses = []

for review in review_averages:
    biz_ref = review.get('business_ref')
    if biz_ref in review_biz_ids:
        avg_rating = float(review.get('avg_rating', 0))
        total_rating += avg_rating
        rating_count += 1
        rated_businesses.append((biz_ref, avg_rating))

avg_rating = total_rating / rating_count if rating_count > 0 else 0

print(f"Businesses with ratings found: {rating_count}")
print(f"Average rating: {avg_rating:.2f}")

# Verify with a few examples
print("\nSample businesses in top category with ratings:")
for i, (biz_ref, rating) in enumerate(rated_businesses[:3]):
    biz_id = biz_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    biz_name = next((b['name'] for b in cc_businesses if b['business_id'] == biz_id), 'Unknown')
    print(f"  {biz_name}: {rating:.2f}")

result = {
    'top_category': top_category,
    'business_count': top_count,
    'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 2)
}

print('\n__RESULT__:', json.dumps(result))

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load the data
biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Total businesses with credit cards:', len(biz))

# Extract categories - simple string parsing
all_categories = []
cat_counts = Counter()

for b in biz:
    desc = b.get('description', '')
    
    # Find the category list in description
    # Look for keywords that indicate categories
    
    cats = []
    
    # Method 1: Find text after "services in"
    if 'services in' in desc:
        start = desc.find('services in') + 12
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Method 2: Find text after "including"
    elif 'including' in desc:
        start = desc.find('including') + 10
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Method 3: Find text after "fields of"
    elif 'fields of' in desc:
        start = desc.find('fields of') + 10
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Clean categories (remove 'and' from beginning if present)
    clean_cats = []
    for c in cats:
        c = c.strip()
        if c.startswith('and '):
            c = c[4:]
        c = c.rstrip('.')
        if len(c) > 2:
            clean_cats.append(c)
            all_categories.append(c)
            cat_counts[c] += 1

print('Categories found', len(cat_counts))
for cat, cnt in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(cat + ':', cnt)

top = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print('\nTop:', top[0], 'with', top[1], 'businesses')

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

# Load the data
biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Total businesses with credit cards:', len(biz))

# Extract categories - simple string parsing
all_categories = []
cat_counts = Counter()

for b in biz:
    desc = b.get('description', '')
    
    # Find the category list in description
    # Look for keywords that indicate categories
    
    cats = []
    
    # Method 1: Find text after "services in"
    if 'services in' in desc:
        start = desc.find('services in') + 12
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Method 2: Find text after "including"
    elif 'including' in desc:
        start = desc.find('including') + 10
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Method 3: Find text after "fields of"
    elif 'fields of' in desc:
        start = desc.find('fields of') + 10
        end = desc.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = desc[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            cats = [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
    
    # Clean categories (remove 'and' from beginning if present)
    clean_cats = []
    for c in cats:
        c = c.strip()
        if c.startswith('and '):
            c = c[4:]
        c = c.rstrip('.')
        if len(c) > 2:
            clean_cats.append(c)
            all_categories.append(c)
            cat_counts[c] += 1

print('Categories found', len(cat_counts))
for cat, cnt in cat_counts.most_common(10):
    print(cat + ':', cnt)

top = cat_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print('\nTop:', top[0], 'with', top[1], 'businesses')

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

var_functions.query_db:2.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Businesses:', len(biz))
print('Reviews summaries:', len(rev))

categories = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    
    # Extract after key phrases
    text = ''
    if 'services in' in d:
        m = d.find('services in')
        text = d[m+12:]
    elif 'including' in d:
        m = d.find('including')
        text = d[m+10:]
    elif 'fields of' in d:
        m = d.find('fields of')
        text = d[m+10:]
    
    if '.' in text:
        text = text.split('.')[0]
        
    if ',' in text:
        parts = text.split(',')
        for p in parts:
            p = p.strip()
            if p.startswith('and '):
                p = p[4:]
            if len(p) > 1:
                categories.append(p)

counter = Counter(categories)
print('Categories count:', len(counter))
for c,i in counter.most_common(10):
    print(c, i)

top = counter.most_common(1)[0]
print('\nTop:', top)

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json, re
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Businesses:', len(biz))
print('Reviews summaries:', len(rev))

categories = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    
    # Extract after key phrases
    text = ''
    if 'services in' in d:
        m = d.find('services in')
        text = d[m+12:]
    elif 'including' in d:
        m = d.find('including')
        text = d[m+10:]
    elif 'fields of' in d:
        m = d.find('fields of')
        text = d[m+10:]
    
    if '.' in text:
        text = text.split('.')[0]
        
    if ',' in text:
        parts = text.split(',')
        for p in parts:
            p = p.strip()
            if p.startswith('and '):
                p = p[4:]
            if len(p) > 1:
                categories.append(p)

counter = Counter(categories)
print('Categories count:', len(counter))
for c,i in counter.most_common(10):
    print(c, i)

top = counter.most_common(1)[0]
print('\nTop:', top)
