Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Data loaded')
print('Businesses:', len(biz))
print('Review summaries:', len(rev))

# Simple extraction
cat_count = Counter()
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    start = -1
    # Find positions manually
    si = d.find('services in')
    inc = d.find('including')
    fo = d.find('fields of')
    
    if si > 0:
        start = si + 12
    elif inc > 0:
        start = inc + 10
    elif fo > 0:
        start = fo + 10
    
    if start > 0:
        end = d.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = d[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                if p.startswith('and'):
                    p = p[4:]
                p = p.rstrip('.')
                if len(p) > 2:
                    cat_count[p] += 1

print('Categories found:', len(cat_count))
print('Top categories:')
for cat, cnt in cat_count.most_common(10):
    print('  ', cat, cnt)

top_category = cat_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
top_business_count = cat_count.most_common(1)[0][1]
print('\nTop category:', top_category)
print('Business count:', top_business_count)

# Find business IDs with this category
business_ids = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    bid = b.get('business_id','')
    if top_category in d:
        business_ids.append(bid)

print('Business IDs found:', len(business_ids))
print('Sample IDs:', business_ids[:5])

# Convert to review IDs
review_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in business_ids]

# Calculate average rating
total = 0.0
count = 0
for r in rev:
    if r.get('business_ref') in review_ids:
        total += float(r.get('avg_rating'))
        count += 1

avg_rating = round(total / count, 2) if count > 0 else 0
print('Rated businesses:', count)
print('Average rating:', avg_rating)

result = {}
result['top_category'] = top_category
result['business_count'] = top_business_count
result['avg_rating'] = avg_rating

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Data loaded')
print('Businesses:', len(biz))
print('Review summaries:', len(rev))

# Simple extraction
cat_count = Counter()
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    start = -1
    # Find positions manually
    si = d.find('services in')
    inc = d.find('including')
    fo = d.find('fields of')
    
    if si > 0:
        start = si + 12
    elif inc > 0:
        start = inc + 10
    elif fo > 0:
        start = fo + 10
    
    if start > 0:
        end = d.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = d[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                if p.startswith('and'):
                    p = p[4:]
                p = p.rstrip('.')
                if len(p) > 2:
                    cat_count[p] += 1

print('Categories found:', len(cat_count))
print('Top categories:')
for cat, cnt in cat_count.most_common(10):
    print('  ', cat, cnt)

top_category = cat_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
top_business_count = cat_count.most_common(1)[0][1]
print('\nTop category:', top_category)
print('Business count:', top_business_count)

# Find business IDs with this category
business_ids = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    bid = b.get('business_id','')
    if top_category in d:
        business_ids.append(bid)

print('Business IDs found:', len(business_ids))
print('Sample IDs:', business_ids[:5])

# Convert to review IDs
review_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in business_ids]

# Calculate average rating
total = 0.0
count = 0
for r in rev:
    if r.get('business_ref') in review_ids:
        total += float(r.get('avg_rating'))
        count += 1

avg_rating = round(total / count, 2) if count > 0 else 0
print('Rated businesses:', count)
print('Average rating:', avg_rating)

result = {}
result['top_category'] = top_category
result['business_count'] = top_business_count
result['avg_rating'] = avg_rating

print('\n__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Starting analysis')

# Extract categories from descriptions
categories = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    # Find where categories start
    si = d.find('services in')
    inc = d.find('including')
    fo = d.find('fields of')
    
    start = 0
    if si > 0: start = si + 12
    elif inc > 0: start = inc + 10
    elif fo > 0: start = fo + 10
    
    if start > 0:
        end = d.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = d[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                if p.startswith('and '): p = p[4:]
                p = p.rstrip('.')
                if len(p) > 2: categories.append(p)

cat_counter = Counter(categories)
print('Categories found:', len(cat_counter))

if not cat_counter:
    result = {'error': 'No categories found'}
else:
    top_cat, top_cnt = cat_counter.most_common(1)[0]
    
    # Get business IDs with this category
    biz_ids = []
    for b in biz:
        if top_cat in b.get('description',''):
            biz_ids.append(b.get('business_id'))
    
    rev_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in biz_ids]
    
    # Calculate rating
    total = 0
    cnt = 0
    for r in rev:
        if r.get('business_ref') in rev_ids:
            total += float(r.get('avg_rating'))
            cnt += 1
    
    avg_rating = round(total / cnt, 2) if cnt > 0 else 0
    
    result = {}
    result['top_category'] = top_cat
    result['business_count'] = top_cnt
    result['avg_rating'] = avg_rating
    result['rated_businesses'] = cnt

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.

val_args.code:
import json
from collections import Counter

biz = var_functions.query_db:82
rev = var_functions.query_db:64

print('Starting analysis')

# Extract categories from descriptions
categories = []
for b in biz:
    d = b.get('description','')
    # Find where categories start
    si = d.find('services in')
    inc = d.find('including')
    fo = d.find('fields of')
    
    start = 0
    if si > 0: start = si + 12
    elif inc > 0: start = inc + 10
    elif fo > 0: start = fo + 10
    
    if start > 0:
        end = d.find('.', start)
        if end > start:
            text = d[start:end]
            parts = text.split(',')
            for p in parts:
                p = p.strip()
                if p.startswith('and '): p = p[4:]
                p = p.rstrip('.')
                if len(p) > 2: categories.append(p)

cat_counter = Counter(categories)
print('Categories found:', len(cat_counter))

if not cat_counter:
    result = {'error': 'No categories found'}
else:
    top_cat, top_cnt = cat_counter.most_common(1)[0]
    
    # Get business IDs with this category
    biz_ids = []
    for b in biz:
        if top_cat in b.get('description',''):
            biz_ids.append(b.get('business_id'))
    
    rev_ids = [bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in biz_ids]
    
    # Calculate rating
    total = 0
    cnt = 0
    for r in rev:
        if r.get('business_ref') in rev_ids:
            total += float(r.get('avg_rating'))
            cnt += 1
    
    avg_rating = round(total / cnt, 2) if cnt > 0 else 0
    
    result = {}
    result['top_category'] = top_cat
    result['business_count'] = top_cnt
    result['avg_rating'] = avg_rating
    result['rated_businesses'] = cnt

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:70.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
